Second ability would have to be limited to once per turn.

cards.notes:
Tylor Jordon
I didn't see the other post, but I feel like I gotta point out that Evolution is worded in a way that like, I understand, but grammatically is a bit of a mess. I realize that you scooped the verbiage straight from Mutate, but here's how I'd write the reminder text.
Evolution [X] {cost} (You can cast this spell for its Evolution cost by putting it over target creature you control named [X]. If you do, this creature gains the abilities of all cards under it.)
There's something about "They evolve into this creature plus all abilities under it" that sounds really dumb (I think mutate sounds stupid, too).
Also with Metapod, I think wizards would write its ability as, "{0}: Prevent the next 2 damage that would be dealt to this creature. Activate this ability only once each turn." There are no abilities in magic that say "prevent the first X damage" so it sounds kind of out of place.
I love Butterfree's design, though.

cards.notes:
My Pokémon set update for numbers 0001 - 0101 almost finished with the Kanto dex!
Based on feedback some big changes have happened so this is all of them in one place again.
Some of the changes...
Pokémon as a creature type
Mega and Gigantamax remain supertypes with Mega having it's own "Legend" rule effect where you can only have one on your field at a time.
Ice type Pokémon are now Snow 
Weather effects - a bit of an experiment to see how people react. Now like the game some Pokémon can change the weather and other gain boosts under certain weather. Weather works like the current Day and Night cycle in Magic but don't have a set way to switch them and other than impacting cards that care about them does not impact them game for simplicity sake.

Feedback welcome as always
